mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 52% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side weakness that suppresses corporate earnings expectations, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during late-cycle phases produce positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 2.0 percent year-over-year confirms disinflationary pressure, prompting market participants to price in imminent rate cuts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings below 2.0% fail to precede positive 5-day returns in > 65% of observations, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during supply-shock regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates signals structural labor market deterioration, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns with > 55% accuracy, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows, or 20-day forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in industrial production during periods of elevated inflation indicate stagflationary supply constraints, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism breaks down when monetary policy pivots to easing.
